chapter_title.en: The Merits of the Euphrates River, Drinking from Its Water, and Performing Ghusl in It

text: 6 وَ حَدَّثَنِي عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ (رحمه الله) عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ (ع) قَالَ الْفُرَاتُ سَيِّدُ الْمِيَاهِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ

en.sayyid-mohsen-al-husayni-al-milani: 6. ‘Ali ibn Ḥusain narrated to me from Sa’d ibn ‘Abdillāh, from Aḥmad ibn Muḥammad ibn ‘Isā, from Ḥasan (ibn ‘Ali ibn Faďďāl), from ‘Isā ibn ‘Abdillāh ibn Muḥammad ibn ‘Umar, from his father, from his grandfather, who said: ‘Ali, (the Commander of the Believers (a.s.)), said: The Euphrates (River) is the master of the waters in this life and in the Hereafter.
